items.title: Factory worker, 23, survives 30‑foot fall at Ipoh cement plant, suffers minor injuries
items.summary: IPOH, July 4 — A factory worker survived after falling 30 feet (9.144 metres) from a cement factory building in Persiaran Tasek, Tasek Industrial Estate here yesterday. Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the 23-year-old man sustained only minor injuries in the incident. He said the department received a call on the accident at 4.50pm and personnel from the Ipoh Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene six minutes later.

items.title: Building safer environments, supporting vulnerable individuals a shared responsibility
items.summary: While some may be linked to mental health challenges, others may involve accidents, negligence, unsafe environments or other circumstances. Mental health is undoubtedly an important issue that deserves greater attention. Nevertheless, we should continue to strengthen mental health awareness, improve access to counselling services and encourage early intervention for individuals experiencing emotional or psychological distress.

items.title: #NSTviral: Freezing offices spark debate among workers
items.summary: Many users said they move between Malaysia's heat and overly cold offices, often spending the workday shivering indoors. User @amirasri91 said it was difficult to reconcile sustainability messages with excessively cold workplaces. Another user, @tak.judge, said offices should aim for comfort rather than extreme temperatures.

items.title: Nearly 200,000 diesel drivers tap Budi Madani scheme in three days, MyKad rollout hailed smooth
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 4 — Nearly 200,000 private diesel vehicle owners have benefited from the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) programme within the first three days of its implementation, said Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican. Johan Mahmood said the implementation involves the usage of MyKad for individuals and fleet cards for the commercial sector, with only minor accessibility issues reported, similar to what was experienced during the rollout of previous targeted subsidy programmes. “So far, the feedback we have received indicates that the implementation has been smooth, whether using MyKad or fleet cards.

items.title: Huge crowds expected as Khamenei funeral ceremonies open in Iran
items.summary: TEHERAN: Huge crowds of Iranians were expected to pay their final respects to supreme leader Ali Khamenei on Saturday at the start of marathon funeral ceremonies intended to serve as a show of strength to the Islamic republic's foes. Iranian authorities say they anticipate between 15 and 20 million participants in Teheran alone over the next three days for tributes to the man who ran the country for three-and-a-half decades. Six days of funeral ceremonies are planned to commemorate Khamenei, who ruled the Islamic republic as its number one from 1989 until his killing aged 86 on the first day of the US-Israeli war with Iran on Feb 28.

items.title: Trump pardons 11, most for violating Clean Air Act
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump pardoned 11 people on Friday, a White House official said, all but two of whom had been convicted of violating the Clean Air Act by modifying or disabling emissions controls on trucks. Trump posted on Truth Social about six of the men, who he said had been prosecuted under President Joe Biden's administration for "fixing their car." His administration in February repealed a scientific finding that greenhouse gas emissions endanger human health, and eliminated federal tailpipe emissions standards for cars and trucks.

items.title: President-elect Fujimori hails 'new chapter' for Peru
items.summary: LIMA: Peru's conservative president-elect Keiko Fujimori on Friday said a "new chapter" was beginning as the highest electoral authority confirmed her narrow victory. The formal declaration of Fujimori's win brings one of Peru's tightest leadership contests of all time to a close and ushers in the Andean nation's ninth president in a decade. "I proclaim Miss Keiko Sofia Fujimori Higuchi as President of the Republic, and Mister Luis Fernando Galarreta Velarde as First Vice President of the Republic," election chief Roberto Burneo stated at a ceremony in Lima.

items.title: McDonald's, bus station convert into Venezuela quake clinics
items.summary: LA GUAIRA, (Venezuela): Four bags of IV fluid hang from the ceiling of a McDonald's converted into a clinic in the aftermath of the two earthquakes in Venezuela, where thousands are flocking to makeshift health centers in the disaster's epicenter. The double earthquake, measuring 7.2 and 7.5 in magnitude, struck La Guaira state, which lacks the capacity for mass hospital care after widespread destruction, scores of collapsed buildings and more than 2,600 deaths. Nearly 4,000 patients have been treated at another makeshift health center set up inside a bus terminal in Catia La Mar.

items.title: Cambodian king returns from China after cancer treatment
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: Cambodia's King Norodom Sihamoni returned home on Friday from China where he was treated for prostate cancer, according to state media, with the palace saying his health was improved. Sihamoni, 73, departed for Beijing for health checks earlier this year and shared his cancer diagnosis in April. The king has been receiving treatment from Chinese doctors in Beijing after undergoing "successful" surgery, according to the Royal Palace in Phnom Penh.

items.title: Trump's massive July 4 firework show raises health alarms
items.summary: That's the question as Washington prepares to break the record for the world's largest fireworks display on the United States' 250th birthday on Saturday. The Trump administration's "Freedom250" project has hired Pennsylvania-based Pyrotecnico to launch more than 850,000 fireworks shells across 10 sites around the Lincoln Memorial and Potomac River, starting at 10.30pm and lasting 40 minutes. - 'FRIGHTENING' NUMBERS - "Fireworks are a tried-and-true American tradition," Jodi Dague, Pyrotecnico's director of marketing, told AFP.

items.title: One dead, three injured in squid jigging boat capsize off Kuala Nerus
items.summary: District police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the incident occurred at about 9pm when the boat, carrying 12 people, including the skipper, capsized at sea. He said the victim was identified as crew member Ahmad Nasaruddin Mohmad Jalil, 36. "Three people were injured while eight passengers and the skipper escaped unharmed," he said when contacted today.

items.title: Stilettos, beer and sex: Pattaya's enduring notoriety returns to the spotlight
items.summary: PATTAYA: Sitting on a stool under purple neon lights, Emily waited for clients in the back of a bar in Pattaya, a notorious Thai seaside resort back in the spotlight over a sordid murder. The sex worker the other so-called bar girls call "Mum" arrived in Pattaya more than 20 years ago, but remains on her guard with every encounter. Emily said she was not shocked.

items.title: Drug gangs use social media to recruit Thai air crew as couriers
items.summary: BANGKOK: Early in the morning on June 18, a message from an unknown account slipped into the TikTok inbox of a flight attendant in Bangkok with a series of questions: "Are you flying to Australia? The 30-year-old, who flies for a regional budget carrier, ignored the message and forgot about it — until Tuesday, when a Thai Airways flight attendant was charged with importing more than one kilogramof heroin into Australia hidden in several tote bags. The unknown account — named "Powder is Powder" in Thai — was linked to drug trafficking networks that create fake social media accounts to find people to move illicit substances across borders, said Areepak Ngernbamroong, a spokesperson for Thailand's Office of the Narcotics Control Board.

items.title: Nurse defies Buddhist tradition to aid monks after crash kills 10
items.summary: BANGKOK: A nurse in Thailand challenged Buddhist customs when she stepped in to treat the victims of a car accident that killed 10 monks. Minutes later Wiwat Laonoi stepped in to help, despite the Buddhist taboo in which women cannot touch monks and vice versa. Five monks died at the scene on Thursday, and another five died later in hospital.

items.title: Indonesia warns farmers to prepare for El Nino-driven dry weather
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's weather agency warned farmers to adjust their farming activities to mitigate risks stemming from the extreme dry weather expected this year, according to a government statement on Friday. As of June, around 38 per cent of Indonesia's seasonal zones have entered the dry season, while 47 per cent of the zones have experienced lower than normal rain volume, Ardhasena Sopaheluwakan, an official with the country's weather agency (BMKG) said in the statement. Sopaheluwakan urged farmers to make adjustments to their farming activities due to the intensifying El Nino effects.

items.title: Suhakam seeks regular spot checks on detention centres [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) wants regular, unannounced access to Immigration detention depots. Section 4(3) of the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia Act 1999 requires visits to places of detention to comply with the legal and administrative procedures governing those facilities. Under Section 4(2)(d) of the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia Act 1999, Suhakam is mandated to visit places of detention and make recommendations subject to procedures under the relevant laws.

items.title: India's Modi to visit Indonesia, Australia, New Zealand next week
items.summary: NEW DELHI: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi will visit Indonesia, Australia and New Zealand next week, as New Delhi seeks to strengthen strategic ties in the region. The July 6-11 tour comes after Modi's visits to Mauritius in March last year and Seychelles last month, reflecting what India's external affairs ministry described as a shift in diplomatic focus to the eastern maritime region under its 'Act East' policy. Modi will review cooperation across a range of sectors during his visit to Indonesia, including maritime security, defence, trade and cultural ties, Tandon said.

items.title: LRT3: Malaysia is asking the wrong question
items.summary: LETTERS: As the debate over LRT3 continues, much of the public discussion has centred on one issue: how much the project cost and whether enough money was saved through rationalisation. Every ringgit of public money should be spent responsibly. That is the true purpose of public transport.

items.title: Malaysia, Australia boost strategic defence ties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and Australia have deepened their defence cooperation, reinforcing collaboration in strategic technology, industry and research. The development follows a three-day working visit by Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin to Australia, during which a strategic collaboration was formalised between Weststar Defence Industries Sdn Bhd and Australian aerospace and defence company Rocket Technologies International Pty Ltd (RTI) at the National Convention Centre in Canberra. Khaled said the partnership marked a significant step towards strengthening Malaysia's defence industrial capabilities, particularly in rocket and missile technologies, in line with the country's long-term ambition to build sovereign defence capacity.

items.title: Johor polls: Amirudin defends PH manifesto against copycat claims
items.summary: KLUANG: PKR vice‑president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari has dismissed claims that Pakatan Harapan's (PH) manifesto for the 16th Johor State Election was copied, saying it was developed by coalition leaders over several months. Also present were PKR vice‑president R. Ramanan, Amanah secretary‑general Faiz Fadzil, PH Machap candidate Nur Hafiz Roslan, PH Benut candidate Abd Razak Ismail and PH Layang‑Layang candidate Guna Balakrishnan. On criticism that PH's housing target was unrealistic, Amirudin, who is also Selangor Menteri Besar, said the target reflected current needs.

items.title: RMN to phase out KD Mahawangsa and KD Sri Indera Sakti
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) will retire its two multi-purpose command and support ships (MPCSS) by 2030. RMN chief Admiral Tan Sri Zulhelmy Ithnain told the New Straits Times that the ships — KD Mahawangsa and KD Sri Indera Sakti — are scheduled for decommissioning in 2029 and 2030 respectively under the 15-to-5 transformation programme. "It will be replaced by the navy's multi-role support ship (MRSS) programme.

items.title: Retail sales seen rebounding in Q2 after soft Q1
items.summary: Retail Group Malaysia (RGM) projects retail sales growth of 4.8 per cent in Q2 against 3.7 per cent growth in Q1, when spending momentum came in below expectations despite festive, policy and tourism tailwinds. Despite the expected Q2 recovery, RGM trimmed its full-year 2026 retail sales growth forecast to 3.8 per cent from four per cent previously, citing softer consumer purchasing power. Q1 Growth Misses Expectations Retail sales in Q1 grew 3.7 per cent YoY, missing RGM's earlier forecast of 4.4 per cent.

items.title: Navy sails on despite ageing fleet
items.summary: Its chief, Admiral Tan Sri Zulhelmy Ithnain, told the New Straits Times that the navy assesses its readiness through force availability, deployment sustainability and mission effectiveness. "We are also implementing a concentric deterrence framework, which provides layered defence across three operational zones," he said. He said the navy is also developing drone capabilities, including unmanned surface vessels and aerial vehicles, to support surveillance and maritime security operations.

items.title: Dashcams are catching it all, but experts say footage alone won’t end road rage in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — From dangerous confrontations to motorists being chased or forced to stop, images of road rage have surfaced on social media with growing frequency in recent months, raising concerns over an alarming pattern of aggressive driving on Malaysian roads. An entrepreneur, Silver Low Yue Seng, 29, said he still vividly remembers the terrifying ordeal of narrowly escaping a road accident while driving to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) recently. Low said he was travelling from Kuala Lumpur to KLIA via the E6 North–South Expressway Central Link (ELITE) when he encountered an older woman who he believed was having difficulty driving.

items.title: Mexican authorities identify remains of kidnapped journalist, arrest four police officers
items.summary: MEXICO: Mexican authorities have discovered the body of a journalist who was kidnapped by armed men last month, state prosecutors reported on Friday. Two men carrying assault weapons and wearing what appeared to be government uniforms broke into Roxana Guzman's home in early June and abducted her in an incident captured in a chilling video recording. Authorities discovered her remains in a house in Veracruz in eastern Mexico – where Guzman had lived and worked – the state prosecutor announced in a statement.

items.title: Wildfire menaces Spanish tourist hotspot, Portugal seeks help
items.summary: BARCELONA: Hundreds of Spanish firefighters battled an expanding wildfire near the tourist hotspot of the Costa Brava on Friday as Portugal sought outside help to combat forest fires in the north. Strong winds fanned the flames and prompted the Catalan regional authorities to ask residents of 10 municipalities to stay at home, including the popular Platja d'Aro beach resort. Regional emergency services said around 150 people had been evacuated, including some 70 children from a holiday camp.

items.title: Two deaths, rising sightings: Are Sabah’s crocodiles getting too close for comfort?
items.summary: LAHAD DATU, July 3 — Fears over increase in crocodile attacks are leaving residents from Beaufort to Lahad Datu worried for their safety. “Crocodile sightings and encounters continue to the reported in Tungku areas. On Monday, a 12-year-old boy was found dead, believed attacked by crocodile at Sungai Tegupi.

items.title: Healthy, active, and gaining weight: Taiping council says Malaysia’s three elephants envoys living the good life in Japan’s Tennoji Zoo
items.summary: IPOH, July 3 — Three female elephants, Dara, Amoi and Kelat (DAK), housed at Tennoji Zoo in Japan, continue to show positive progress, with each recording weight gains while remaining healthy and active. Taiping Municipal Council (MPT) president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan said the latest report received from Tennoji Zoo showed that Kelat had gained 260 kilogrammes, while Dara and Amoi recorded weight increases of 35 kg and 30 kg respectively compared with their previous weights. The three elephants, now housed at Tennoji Zoo, are under an international cooperation agreement between Tennoji Zoo and Zoo Taiping & Night Safari, represented by MPT, signed on May 19, 2022 and Oct 6, 2022.

items.title: ‘I live in Larkin, I know Larkin’s demographics’: BN’s Mohd Hairi says being a local boy is his secret weapon
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Mohd Hairi Mad Shah is staking his campaign on his local roots and firsthand knowledge of the community as he seeks to defend the Larkin seat in the 16th Johor state election. Mohd Hairi, who also serves as the state Exco member for Youth, Sports, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives, said his experience as a state government executive strengthens his service record and makes him worthy of continued trust from constituents. N44 Larkin: 76,662 voters Norsinah Abu (Bersama) Suhaizan Kayat (PH-Amanah) Mohd Hairi Mad Shah (BN-Umno) (2022: Mohd Hairi Mad Shah: BN-Umno: Maj: 6,178) — Bernama

items.title: Avoid the parking nightmare: Penang rolls out free hospital shuttle, now serves 600 people a day
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 4 — The Penang Island City Council (MBPP) has allocated RM900,000 annually for the free Central Area Transit (CAT) shuttle bus service linking Komtar with Penang Hospital (HPP), three private hospitals and several healthcare facilities in the city centre. MBPP Engineering Director Cheah Chin Kooi said the service aims to encourage greater use of public transport, reduce reliance on private vehicles, and help ease traffic congestion and parking shortages around the hospital area. Also present were Rapid Bus Sdn Bhd Northern Region head Mohd Amir Abd Halim, HPP director Dr Goh Hin Kwang and Penang Women's Development Corporation chief executive officer Datuk Ong Bee Leng.

items.title: BN candidate says Senggarang housing and tourism plans will continue if re-elected
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 3 — Senggarang state seat incumbent Mohd Yusla Ismail wants to continue the initiatives planned for the area if given the mandate once again in the Johor state election, with a focus on home ownership among younger people and the development of the tourism sector. Besides housing, Mohd Yusla said he also wants to continue efforts to develop the local tourism sector, which he believes can stimulate the local economy. — Bernama N24: Senggarang — 38,576 voters Datuk Mohd Rashid Hasnon (PN-Bersatu) Onn Abu Bakar (PH-PKR) Mohd Yusla Ismail (BN-Umno) (2022: Mohd Yusla Ismail: BN-Umno: Majority: 3,912) — Bernama

items.title: A new chapter begins for SJKT Ladang Sungai Muar as Segamat school relocation moves forward
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — The proposed relocation of Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Tamil (SJKT) Ladang Sungai Muar in Segamat is currently at the land ownership stage, with the process being handled in collaboration with the Segamat Land and Mines Office. Segamat Member of Parliament R. Yuneswaran said this was confirmed during an engagement session with the school, which was also attended by Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek in Segamat today. Yuneswaran said that since being elected as Segamat MP in 2022, he has consistently championed the relocation of the school due to safety concerns, its distance from the local community and infrastructure issues.

items.title: BN vs Pakatan: Here’s what Ahmad Zahid thinks of his rival’s Johor election promises
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Barisan Nasional (BN) respects Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) manifesto for the Johor state election, said BN Chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Deputy Prime Minister said that as a democratic nation, BN welcomes proposals from parties contesting the election. Also present were Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi and BN candidate for the Larkin state seat Mohd Hairi Mad Shah.

items.title: Home Ministry: Foreign begging cases may involve syndicates, 42 detained in KL operations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Begging activities involving foreigners, especially children, may involve organised crime and are believed to be linked to syndicates, said the Ministry of Home Affairs (KDN). It said from 2025 until May 31 this year, the Immigration Department carried out 836 enforcement operations in Kuala Lumpur and surrounding areas, inspecting 10,609 individuals. The ministry said to enhance enforcement, the Immigration Department has increased integrated operations with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL), and improved intelligence sharing and analysis to identify syndicate networks, modus operandi, and begging hotspots.

items.title: Anwar: JS‑SEZ on track with federal-state cooperation, urges leaders not to politicise issue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) is progressing according to plan and involves close cooperation between the federal and state governments, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. “This is a federal government project, naturally in collaboration with the state government. The matter of announcements and signing is between the Prime Minister of Malaysia and the Prime Minister of Singapore,” he told reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid As-Sodiqin, Taman Kobena, here today.

items.title: Zahid urges all parties to avoid old, unrelated issues in Johor campaign
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has reminded all parties to stop playing up old issues unrelated to Johor during the state election campaign period, to ensure a mature and respectful political contest. Ahmad Zahid also denied claims that BN is overconfident in facing the state election, saying the party considers itself the underdog, based on changes in the state’s political landscape. BN is contesting all 56 seats in the 16th Johor state election.

items.title: Penang airport expansion records strong progress, project slated for 2028 completion, says exco
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 3 — The expansion of the Penang International Airport is progressing according to schedule, with several key work packages recording encouraging progress and the overall project expected to be completed in 2028. Penang Infrastructure, Transport and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said Work Package One (WP01), comprising the construction of support buildings, utility relocation, infrastructure works and the Remote Digital Virtual Tower (RDVT), had achieved 100 per cent completion. Zairil said the Penang International Airport expansion is among the Federal Government’s strategic initiatives to increase the airport’s annual passenger handling capacity from 6.5 million to 12 million.

items.title: Perdana Park returns: Tanjung Aru’s favourite sunset spot reopens this Saturday
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 3 — Perdana Park in Tanjung Aru is making its comeback tomorrow, after a prolonged and sporadic closure that began nearly two years ago. Visitors can access the park daily from 7am to 7pm. DBKK warned that several areas remain off-limits while safety assessments continue.

items.title: BN confident of victory, targets over 40 seats to retain Johor, says Ahmad Maslan
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is confident of winning the Johor polls and forming the next state government to ensure continuity in the state’s administration. Johor Umno deputy liaison committee chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said BN was on course to achieve its target of winning more than 40 of the 56 seats in the State Legislative Assembly. He said his confidence was based on his observations and assessment after assisting campaign efforts in 25 of Johor’s 26 parliamentary constituencies.

items.title: MOH overhauls emergency triage system with new five‑tier Malaysian Triage Scale to improve assessments and cut delays
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) is revamping the triage system at public hospital emergency departments with the Malaysian Triage Scale (MTS) 2022 to assess patients more accurately and cut treatment delays. Under the new MTS 2022, MOH has upgraded the previous three-tier colour-coded system under the Malaysian Triage Category (2011) to a more refined five-tier scale, ranging from Level 1 (Resuscitation) to Level 5 (Routine). The Ministry said the new system enhances assessment precision by dividing the process into Primary Triage (quick first-glance evaluation) and Secondary Triage (detailed assessment with vital signs).

items.title: Anwar: US pardon won’t get Jho Low off the hook in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has maintained that Malaysia will not alter its position on fugitive financier Jho Low, regardless of whether the US government grants him a presidential pardon. Speaking to reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid As-Sodiqin in Cheras, Anwar decoupled Malaysia’s legal stance from any potential clemency in the US, NST reported. Further reports from The Wall Street Journal indicate that Low filed a pardon request in May seeking the dismissal of his US criminal charges.

items.title: Johor expands education reform to religious schools under ‘Sekolah Rintis Bangsa Johor’ initiative, says MB
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — The Johor government is expanding the Sekolah Rintis Bangsa Johor (SRBJ) initiative to religious education with the establishment of the first Sekolah Agama Rintis Bangsa Johor (SARBJ) this year. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the initiative is the brainchild of the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, to comprehensively transform the state’s education system. “Alhamdulillah, the Sekolah Rintis Bangsa Johor initiative is the Regent of Johor’s brainchild.

items.title: PM receives farewell visit from Chief Statistician Uzir Mahidin ahead of retirement, thanks him for years of service
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today received a farewell courtesy visit from Chief Statistician of Malaysia Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin ahead of his mandatory retirement on July 8. In a Facebook post, the Prime Minister expressed his appreciation for Mohd Uzir’s service and dedication in strengthening the national statistical system, which serves as a key foundation for policymaking and Malaysia’s development planning. Anwar also shared photos of the visit, noting that Mohd Uzir was appointed Chief Statistician on February 14, 2017.

items.title: Anwar: Azam Baki’s NFCC role independent of MACC post
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Tan Sri Azam Baki’s membership in the National Anti-Financial Crime Centre (NFCC) Advisory Board has nothing to do with his previous position as the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said Azam’s appointment to the board falls under the authority of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong and remains valid until 2027. Earlier, the National Financial Crime Prevention Centre (NFCC) clarified that Azam is still a member of its Advisory Board.

items.title: Court of Appeal upholds ruling that DAP’s Chong Chieng Jen defamed Sarawak government
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Court of Appeal has unanimously dismissed an appeal by Sarawak DAP chief Chong Chieng Jen, upholding a High Court ruling that he defamed the Sarawak government and the Sarawak Financial Authority (SFA) over claims regarding state funds in 2013. The three-judge panel increased the damages awarded to the state government and the SFA from RM150,000 to RM300,000. Chong said following the decision that he is considering filing an appeal to the Federal Court.

items.title: Five Taiping Prison officers charged over alleged baton attacks on inmates
items.summary: TAIPING, July 3 — Five prison officers were charged in the Magistrate's Court here today with injuring three inmates at Taiping Prison in separate incidents last January. All of the accused — Mohd Hairie Jumri, 40; Saiful Azman Mohamad Ibrahim, 44; Ahmad Rizal Razali, 37; Muhamad Mustakhim Abdul Rahim, 40; and Nor Hafiz Izwan Muhammad Jajam, 31 — pleaded not guilty to the charges. In the court before Magistrate R. Prabakaran, Mohd Hairie and Saiful Azman are jointly charged with intentionally causing hurt to P. Jason Immanuel, 25, using a baton at Hall B, Taiping Prison, Jalan Taming Sari here, between 3pm and 4pm on Jan 17, 2025.

items.title: Shafee tells court Najib’s tax appeal would be moot if LHDN insists on ‘pay first, talk later’
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 3 — Lawyer Tan Sri Shafee Abdullah argued before the Court of Appeal today that failure to stay the Inland Revenue Board’s (LHDN) case against ex-prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak would render his appeal before the Special Commissioners of Income Tax (SCIT) in September “meaningless.” Speaking before a three-member bench chaired by Justice Datuk Alwi Abdul Wahab, Shafee contended that proceeding with the bankruptcy action would be “akin to entering the boxing ring with both hands tied,” Free Malaysia Today reported. The dispute centres on an additional tax assessment of RM1.69 billion for the 2011 to 2017 period, stemming from an alleged RM8 billion donation that Najib maintains was used for political and welfare activities. This hearing follows a November 17 ruling last year, in which the High Court dismissed applications by Najib and his son, Nazifuddin, to stay bankruptcy proceedings over unpaid tax arrears of RM1.69 billion and RM37.6 million, respectively.

items.title: Home minister: Police working with Interpol to locate Na’imah, children in Europe in ongoing probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) are tracking the whereabouts of the late Tun Daim Zainuddin’s widow, Toh Puan Na’imah Abdul Khalid, and her children, who are believed to be in Europe, to assist in an ongoing police investigation. “At this stage, PDRM is actively working to locate the late Tun Daim’s widow, Toh Puan Na’imah Abdul Khalid, and her children to assist in completing the ongoing police investigation. Saifuddin Nasution said PDRM had opened an investigation paper following Sentul Police Report No.

items.title: 4WD slips off deteriorating Baram logging bridge, renewing concerns over rural road safety
items.summary: MIRI, July 3 — A near-tragic incident involving a four-wheel-drive (4WD) vehicle on a deteriorating logging bridge along the road to SK Long Pelutan has renewed concerns over poor road conditions in the Baram interior. The incident occurred on Wednesday when the vehicle reportedly slipped off while crossing a damaged bridge along the route linking Long Lama and Miri, a road frequently used by teachers travelling to and from SK Long Pelutan. “The road conditions at the scene are extremely poor and dangerous.

items.title: Ex‑kindergarten teacher charged with laundering RM133,550 linked to alleged child‑relocation scheme to New Zealand
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 3 — A former kindergarten teacher pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to three counts of money laundering involving RM133,550 in cash and gold jewellery allegedly linked to a scheme promising to facilitate a child’s relocation to New Zealand last year. According to the charges, Venmani is accused of receiving proceeds from unlawful activities in three separate transactions involving RM83,550, RM50,000, two gold chains and a gold pendant from a 39-year-old local woman. The charges were framed under Section 4(1)(b) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (Act 613), punishable under Section 4(1) of the same Act.

items.title: Anwar boosts taxi replacement fund by RM10m, says dedicated Proton S70 financing in the works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced an additional RM10 million allocation for the Vehicle Replacement Matching Grant Programme to help taxi drivers replace ageing vehicles. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said the additional funding follows the encouraging response to the initial RM10 million allocation for the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme, announced in Budget 2026. Given the importance of the taxi industry, I have approved an immediate additional RM10 million specifically for taxi reform,” he said at the launch of the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme at Dataran Merdeka today.

items.title: Armizan: Applications open today for diesel‑subsidy scheme for company jeeps, pick‑ups; payments start July 15
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 3 — Applications for the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) have opened today for the land transport sector covering company-owned private vehicles, including jeeps and pick-up trucks, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said. “To qualify, vehicles must be registered as business vehicles under the Company Private Use (AE) class/code in the Road Transport Department’s (JPJ) MySikap system. The government had previously extended SKDS eligibility to two other categories: the public land transport sector and the consumer goods land transport sector.

items.title: PLUS: Smart Lane at Bandar Saujana Putra-Putra Heights to close for five months for maintenance
items.summary: PLUS Malaysia Berhad (PLUS) in a statement today said the temporary closure is to facilitate maintenance works as part of its ongoing efforts to improve the highway infrastructure and ensure the safety of highway users. “Throughout the maintenance works, the existing three lanes at the affected stretch will remain open to maintain traffic flow and to minimise traffic disruptions for highway users,” the statement read. PLUS also encouraged highway users to plan their journeys using the PLUS app for the latest traffic updates and obey all traffic signage and instructions issued by PLUS personnel when passing through the construction zone.
